reasoning: The prediction timeline evaluates whether active regular US military personnel physically enter Iran for combat operations at any point between June 21, 2025, and December 31, 2025. Searches up to late October 2025 reveal that while there was a significant escalation in June 2025 (US aircraft struck Iranian nuclear sites after an Israeli-Iranian war), the official US strategy heavily relies on airpower, missile defense, and avoiding 'boots on the ground'. President Trump and Vice President Vance explicitly stated there is no interest in deploying ground troops or committing to a protracted invasion/occupational force. Furthermore, around September/October 2025, the US reduced troop counts in Iraq and moved aerial refueling assets into the region, indicating a continuation of an air-centric, defensive, or limited strike posture rather than a ground campaign. A full-scale invasion of Iran would require hundreds of thousands of troops, which the US has not mobilized. While special operations or diplomatic activities might hypothetically happen, these are explicitly excluded by the resolution criteria. The possibility of regular troops inadvertently crossing the border for combat operations before the end of 2025 remains non-zero but practically minuscule based on the stated posture and logistical realities. I assess this at 2%.
